name: Linguine with White Clam Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 dozen raw clams, little necks or cherrystones, shucked and chopped
24 fresh clams in their shells, garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a deep, heavy kettle.
2. Add garlic and cook over low heat until golden, about 5 minutes.
3. Combine the reserved clam liquid and enough bottled clam juice to make 3 cups.
4. Add this mixture to the kettle along with the parsley and oregano, and salt and pepper to taste. Cover partially and simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Cook the garnishing clams in a separate pan. Add 1 inch of water, cover, and set the pan over high heat. Shake the pan or stir the clams until they open. Remove the opened clams and reserve them in their shells.
6. In a large pot, bring 4 quarts of water to a boil. Add 1 1/2 tablespoons salt and a little olive oil. Drop the linguine into the boiling water. Cook until tender but still firm.
7. Add chopped clams to the sauce and heat gently.
